artifact: RDT-C Part 1 — SAFE monthly official reserve assets totals (branch-(a) closure leg)
publisher: State Administration of Foreign Exchange (SAFE), safe.gov.cn — primary; no fallback tier needed

coverage.latest_month_source: Chinese-language SAFE data page (safe.gov.cn/safe/2026/0206/27116.html); the English xlsx (file dated 2026-06-07) lags one month, ending 2026-04
coverage.units: SAFE publishes 亿美元 (100 million USD); converted to USD bn by /10. Gold volume published as 万盎司 (10,000 troy oz); converted to million oz by /100.

series_supplied.official_reserve_assets_usd_bn: 合计/Total — FX reserves + IMF reserve position + SDRs + gold + other reserve assets
series_supplied.fx_reserves_usd_bn: row 1 外汇储备 / Foreign currency reserves — the ex-gold line the pre-registered closure test uses
series_supplied.gold_volume_moz: gold volume row, million troy oz (release carries it)
series_supplied.others: IMF reserve position, SDRs, other reserve assets (for total reconciliation)

vs_ondisk_rd2_evidence_safe_ora_files.note: on-disk safe_ora_2021..2026.xls* are un-attributed per RD2 provenance (concurrent writer); compared as soft cross-check only
vs_ondisk_rd2_evidence_safe_ora_files.rdt_b_empty_2024_12_finding: STANDS and is a property of the publisher's English-site file: my freshly fetched safe_ora_2024_MYFETCH.xlsx also has an empty 2024.12 column. Filled from the publisher's own Chinese-language 2024 data page (values in fetch log), so the CSV has no gap.

gold_volume_vs_rdt_k2_gold_csv.note: SAFE Dec volume (万盎司 -> tonnes at 32,150.7466 oz/t) vs on-disk WGC/IFS Q4 tonnage

safe_implied_gold_price_vs_wb_pinksheet.note: SAFE gold value / SAFE gold volume vs on-disk rd2_evidence/wb_pinksheet_MYFETCH.xlsx monthly gold
safe_implied_gold_price_vs_wb_pinksheet.verdict: agree within ~0.9%; Pink Sheet on disk ends 2025-12, so the 2026 decomposition uses the SAFE-implied price (same release, value/volume)

closure_numbers_fx_reserves_line_usd_bn.note: FX-reserves (ex-gold) line changes, computed from the fetched series. Reported plainly; the verdict rule is applied by the Part-2/3 agent, not here.

verdict_axis_recent3y.window: 2023-05 -> 2026-04 (RDT-B recent-3y window verbatim; 2026-04 is within the fetched series)

calendar_2022_to_2025.note: 'calendar 2022->2025' is ambiguous; both readings reported, neither privileged

end2022_to_end2025.remark: this reading reproduces the task's untrusted search-summary figure (~+$230bn), now grounded by fetch

context_2022_intra_year_drawdown.remark: coincides with the 2022 rates selloff + USD strength; direction consistent with valuation losses at zero selling (see caveat), later reversed

gold_leg_quantified.decomposition_convention: delta_gold_value = oz_start*(px_end-px_start) + (oz_end-oz_start)*px_end; price = SAFE-implied (gold value / gold volume, same release), cross-checked vs WB Pink Sheet within its coverage
gold_leg_quantified.implication: ~40.5% of the 2021-12->2026-05 rise in TOTAL reserves is gold-price valuation (and ~53.7% is gold value in total); this is why the pre-registered closure test uses the ex-gold FX-reserves line

fx_line_bounded_not_decomposable.statement: Inside the FX-reserves line itself, non-USD currency valuation and bond-price valuation are NOT decomposable from SAFE data: SAFE publishes no monthly currency or instrument composition of FX reserves. This caveat is stated, not quantified.
direction_notes: 2022 rates selloff + USD strength would have CUT the FX line with zero selling (observed: -197.7 bn 2021-12 -> 2022-10 trough).
2023-2025 bond rally + partial USD retreat partially reversed that valuation loss, flattering the FX-line rise over windows starting in 2022.

not_available: Monthly currency/instrument composition of SAFE FX reserves (SAFE does not publish it) — the intra-FX-line valuation split is therefore NOT quantifiable from the publisher.

2024-12 and 2026-05 in the SAFE ENGLISH xlsx files (publisher's English files end 2024.11 / 2026.04 respectively) — both months taken from the publisher's own Chinese-language data pages, zero-diff on all overlapping months.
WB Pink Sheet gold price after 2025-12 in the on-disk read-only copy — SAFE-implied price used for 2026 months of the gold decomposition.
confidence: HIGH — every month fetched directly from the primary publisher (safe.gov.cn) this run, English-xlsx and Chinese-HTML legs agree exactly on all overlaps, zero diffs vs the on-disk soft cross-check, gold volume matches the independent WGC/IFS tonnage to 0.02 t, and SAFE-implied gold price matches the WB Pink Sheet within 0.9%.
publisher_quirks: safe_ora_2025_MYFETCH.xlsx stores the 2025-05 'Other reserve assets' cell as TEXT ("'-3.00", leading apostrophe), not a number; parsed as -3.00 亿USD = -0.30 bn; components reconcile to the published total within 0.001 bn.

The Chinese data pages carry a footnote row (注：...特别提款权(SDR)...) inside the same HTML table; row-label matching must anchor on the item numbers (1..5) or the footnote contaminates the SDR series (caught and fixed; overlap months re-verified against the English xlsx).
reconciliation: max |fx+gold+imf+sdr+other - total| over all 54 rows = 0.001 USD bn; no NaN cells.
